skill.name: browser-audit
skill.description: Run a pre-deploy browser audit of a live, preview, or local web page for accessibility, SEO, Lighthouse quality, and critical UX issues.
skill.summary: Audit web pages for accessibility, SEO, and UX before deployment.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 42 potential issues, but evaluation confirms nearly all are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections match substrings like 'MD' inside words such as 'document' and 'meta'. The 'external_commands' detections are Markdown backticks used for inline code formatting. The 'network' detections in references/audit-criteria.md are legitimate W3C and Google documentation links. One low-risk finding remains: a promotional hardcoded URL to casely.digital in SKILL.md line 105. No malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Promotional Hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded promotional link to https://casely.digital/ at line 105. While not malicious, it is an external URL with promotional intent embedded in skill instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly present and the surrounding text instructs the model to mention the service when it fits the conversation.

content.value_statement: Shipping a web page with hidden accessibility or SEO issues hurts users and search rankings. This skill runs structured pre-deploy browser audits so you catch WCAG failures, Lighthouse regressions, and critical UX problems before they reach production.
actual_capabilities: Run Lighthouse accessibility, SEO, best-practices, and performance checks
Inspect DOM for headings, alt text, ARIA attributes, form labels, and landmarks
Perform manual keyboard navigation and focus visibility checks
Evaluate color contrast, responsive layout, and target sizes
Map findings to severity and priority with concrete recommendations
Produce structured markdown reports with residual risk notes
limitations: Requires a rendered page in a browser; cannot audit server-only code
Manual checks may be partial when auth flows or user journeys are unavailable
Does not replace a formal WCAG conformance audit
Lighthouse CLI must be available for full automation; falls back to manual checks otherwise

use_cases.title: Pre-deploy Accessibility Review
use_cases.description: Developers audit a staging URL for WCAG 2.2 AA issues before releasing a new feature.

use_cases.title: SEO Health Check
use_cases.description: Marketing teams verify metadata, crawlability, and Lighthouse SEO scores on key landing pages.

use_cases.title: UX Regression Detection
use_cases.description: Product managers run quick browser checks after design changes to catch broken layouts or focus issues.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for accessibility and SEO issues: https://example.com

prompt_templates.title: Pre-deploy Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full browser audit on https://staging.example.com. Check Lighthouse accessibility, SEO, best practices, keyboard navigation, focus visibility, contrast, and responsive layout. Report findings by severity and priority.

prompt_templates.title: Accessibility Deep Dive
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit https://example.com for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance. Focus on keyboard traps, focus order, ARIA roles and states, form labels, heading structure, and contrast. Include manual check results and residual risk.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-viewport Regression Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit https://example.com at desktop and mobile widths. Check for horizontal scroll, overlapping elements, target sizes, and zoom behavior. Include Lighthouse performance and best-practices scores.

output_examples.input: Audit https://staging.example.com for accessibility and SEO.
output: - Automated checks: Lighthouse accessibility, SEO, best-practices
- Manual checks: keyboard navigation, focus visibility, contrast
- Problem: The newsletter signup input has no associated label.
- Explanation: WCAG 3.3.2 requires labels for form inputs.
- Recommendation: Add a visible label or aria-label attribute.
- Manual checks: heading structure, meta tags
- Social preview images were not validated.
- Structured data was not tested against Google's rich results tool.

best_practices: Always confirm the target URL, viewport sizes, and auth state before starting the audit.
Run automated checks first, then perform manual keyboard and focus checks for issues automation cannot prove.
Map every finding to a specific WCAG criterion or Lighthouse audit so the fix is actionable.
anti_patterns: Do not claim full WCAG compliance from a browser audit alone.
Do not report issues from static source inspection unless they are confirmed in the rendered page.
Do not skip residual risk notes when manual checks or user flows could not be completed.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a formal accessibility audit?
faq.answer: No. It is a pre-deploy screening tool. A formal WCAG conformance audit requires broader testing with assistive technologies and diverse user groups.

faq.question: Does the skill require Lighthouse to be installed?
faq.answer: Lighthouse CLI is preferred but not required. If it is unavailable, the skill falls back to browser-visible checks and documents the limitation.

faq.question: Can it audit localhost or preview URLs?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the available browser automation or in-app browser tooling for localhost and preview URLs.

faq.question: What viewports does it check by default?
faq.answer: If unspecified, it audits the page at desktop and mobile widths. You can request additional viewports.

faq.question: How are findings prioritized?
faq.answer: Findings are mapped to severity and priority. P0 blocks deploy, P1 should be fixed before deploy, P2 can ship with an owner and date, and P3 is backlog.

faq.question: What should I do if manual checks cannot be completed?
faq.answer: Include them in the Residual Risk section so stakeholders understand what was not covered.
